title: 10 best herbal essential oils for sleep deprivation
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for sleep deprivation are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms of insomnia and promotes relaxation, allowing individuals to achieve a restful night's sleep. The best herbal essential oils for sleep deprivation are Lavandula angustifolia, Valeriana officinalis, Melissa officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Avena sativa, Anthemis nobilis, Santalum album, Zingiber officinale, Chamomilla recutita, and Ginkgo biloba. Without proper treatment, sleep deprivation can lead to mood swings, decreased productivity, and compromised physical health, ultimately affecting daily life and relationships.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because it has a calming effect on the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The oil contains compounds that have been shown to increase the production of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep-wake cycles. Additionally, valerian essential oils can help slow down the heart rate and blood pressure, creating a sense of tranquility and reducing restlessness, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian essential oil has a sedative and calming effect on the mind and body, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that may be contributing to sleep deprivation.

Muscle relaxation: Valerian oil's muscle-relaxing properties can help soothe restlessness and physical tension that can prevent a person from falling asleep or staying asleep, promoting a deeper and more peaceful sleep.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects: The anxiolytic properties of valerian essential oil can help alleviate anxiety and worry, which are common underlying causes of sleep deprivation, allowing the individual to relax and fall asleep more easily.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have a sedative and calming effect on the nervous system, making them ideal for promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, a compound that helps to reduce stress and anxiety, allowing for a peaceful night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and dry them in a warm dark place for at least two weeks.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine one part of the root powder with four parts of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for eight hours under sunlight or moonlight before filtering and transferring it to dark glass bottles.
Store the finished essential oil in a cool dry place protected from light for at least six months before using it as an aromatherapy treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because of its calming and relaxing properties. The oil's sweet, floral scent promotes a sense of tranquility, reducing stress and anxiety that can prevent a restful night's sleep. It also helps to regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle by promoting melatonin production, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. As a result, English lavender essential oils can be a valuable tool in combating chronic sleep deprivation.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic Properties: Lavender oil's anxiolytic properties help to reduce anxiety and stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep by calming the mind and body.
Sedative Properties: The sedative properties of lavender oil promote relaxation, reduce restlessness, and help to quiet the mind, creating a conducive environment for a good night's sleep.

Calming Effects on the Nervous System: Lavender oil's calming effects on the nervous system can help to regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, reducing insomnia and promoting a deeper, more restful sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lavender plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in linalool, a calming compound that has been traditionally used to treat insomnia and restlessness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning or late afternoon when they are fully open.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 8 hours to remove excess moisture.
Steam distill 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 300ml of distilled water for 4 hours using a clevenger apparatus.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter and collect the clear liquid in a dark glass bottle.
Store the finished essential oil in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for at least 24 hours to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because it has a calming effect on the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The sweet, citrusy scent of lemon balm oil helps to slow down a racing mind, quieting thoughts and worries that often keep people awake at night. By incorporating lemon balm essential oil into a bedtime routine, such as through aromatherapy or topical application, individuals can fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly, waking up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Calming Properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which can help individuals fall asleep faster and stay asleep longer. This is because its calming properties help slow down racing thoughts and quiet a restless mind.

Antioxidant Properties: Lemon balm essential oil contains antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are common causes of sleep disturbances. By reducing inflammation, lemon balm oil can promote a more restful and rejuvenating sleep.

GABA-Enhancing Properties: Lemon balm oil has been shown to increase the production of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep-wake cycles. By increasing GABA levels, lemon balm oil can promote deeper sleep and improve sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant are often used to create a calming and sedative essential oil that can help regulate sleep patterns.
Seeds: Lemon balm seeds are used to produce an essential oil that is said to have a soothing effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves using 10 grams of leaves per 100 milliliters of water for 1 hour.
Collect the essential oil that condenses at the top of the distillation apparatus and filter it through cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Store the filtered melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and use within 6 months of production.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because of its unique blend of compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, which promote relaxation and calmness. The oil's sedative properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, making it easier for individuals to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. Additionally, maypop essential oil has been shown to regulate cortisol levels, allowing the body to transition into a state of deep sleep and wake up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Maypop essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce anxiety and stress, making it easier to fall asleep.

Anxiolytic properties: The oil's anxiolytic compounds, such as flavonoids and glycosides, have been shown to possess anti-anxiety effects, helping to soothe an overactive mind and promote relaxation before sleep.

Muscle relaxant properties: Maypop essential oil has a relaxing effect on the muscles, which can help to reduce physical tension and discomfort that may be hindering sleep, allowing for a deeper and more restful slumber.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that promote relaxation, reducing anxiety and insomnia caused by sleep deprivation.
Seeds: Maypop seeds are rich in omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants, which help regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle and improve sleep quality.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning when dew drops are present on the petals for optimal potency and aroma.
Combine one cup of dried flowers with one gallon of high-proof vodka in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid, steeping for 48 hours to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids and reserving the infused liquid for further processing.
Distill the infused liquid using a water-based distillation method at a ratio of one part plant material to five parts distilled water, collecting essential oils as they separate from the distillate.
Transfer the resulting essential oil to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, storing in a cool dry place for up to six months and using within three months for optimal potency and aroma.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Figwort essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because of its calming and sedative properties, which promote a restful night's sleep. The oil's active ingredients, such as figwort glycosides and iridoid glycosides, have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety levels, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. Additionally, the oil's sweet, earthy aroma can help calm the mind and body, allowing for a more peaceful sleep. By incorporating figwort essential oils into your bedtime routine, you can wake up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Relaxant Properties: Herbal figwort essential oil has sedative and relaxant properties, which can help calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep.

Antioxidant Activity: Figwort essential oil contains antioxidant compounds that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing anxiety that can disrupt sleep.

GABA-Mimetic Effects: Figwort essential oil has been shown to mimic the effects of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep and relaxation, which can help individuals with insomnia or sleep disorders fall asleep faster.
remedy_parts: Roots: Figwort roots contain a high concentration of calcium and magnesium, which help relax muscles and promote a restful night's sleep.
Barks: The barks of the figwort plant contain salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that can help soothe stress and anxiety-related insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh scrophularia nodosa leaves in the early morning when dew still glistening on the plant's surface taking approximately 200 grams of material.
Dry the harvested scrophularia nodosa leaves using a combination of sun drying and machine drying for 24 hours to reduce moisture content to 5%.
Steam distill the dried scrophularia nodosa leaves in a stainless steel still with 500 milliliters of water at atmospheric pressure for 2 hours yielding approximately 10 milliliters of essential oil.
Filter the obtained essential oil through a paper filter and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool place away from direct sunlight for up to one year.

Use the scrophularia nodosa essential oil as needed, mixing 2 drops of the essential oil with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil and applying it topically or inhaling through steam inhalation before bedtime for at least two weeks to achieve noticeable sleep benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because of its calming and soothing properties. When inhaled, it can help to relax the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that often keep individuals awake at night. Additionally, licorice oil's sedative effects can help to regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, promoting a deeper and more restful sleep. Its sweet aroma also helps to calm the senses, creating a peaceful environment conducive to a good night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Herbal licorice essential oil has been shown to have anxiolytic effects, which can help reduce anxiety and stress levels that often contribute to sleep deprivation.

Adaptogenic properties: Licorice root is considered an adaptogen, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and promotes balance in the body's response to stressors, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.

Hormone-regulating properties: Herbal licorice essential oil has been found to regulate hormones such as cortisol and adrenaline, which can become imbalanced with sleep deprivation, leading to insomnia and other sleep disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing glycyrrhizin, the root of the licorice plant has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and reduce stress, making it suitable for sleep deprivation.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and triterpenes that have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to induce a restful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm place for at least 72 hours or until crispy.
Use a high-quality essential oil extractor to distill the dried roots into a golden-colored oil.
Add a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a 1:10 ratio and mix thoroughly for 5 minutes.
Store the final blend of glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because they possess a unique blend of properties that promote relaxation, reduce stress, and calm the mind. The citrusy scent of broad-leaved lime oil has been shown to decrease cortisol levels, easing anxiety and restlessness, while its sedative effects help quiet the mind and induce a state of tranquility. remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the broad-leaved lime plant are rich in sedative compounds that help to calm the mind and body, promoting relaxation and deep sleep.

remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos flowers at dawn when they are fully open and free of dew.
Dry the harvested flowers in small batches using a food dehydrator at 30°c for 24 hours.
Combine 100g of dried flowers with 400ml of high-quality carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture for 12 hours under sunlight or using a low-heat infusion method in a dark glass container.
Filter and decant the essential oil after 48 hours, discarding any sediment or impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because it has a calming and sedative effect on the mind and body. The oil's natural constituents, such as camphor and borneol, work together to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, wild marjoram's sweet and herbaceous aroma can help clear the mind of racing thoughts and promote a sense of tranquility, allowing individuals to drift off to sleep faster and enjoy a restful night's sleep.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried origanum vulgare leaves and stems in the early morning hours for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it thinly on paper bags or clean cloth towels for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried origanum vulgare material using a clevenger apparatus with 1 cup of plant material and 500ml of distilled water at 100°c for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil mixture through coffee filters or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to prevent oxidation and contamination.
Store the final origanum vulgare essential oil in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months after opening, shaking well before use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because of its calming and relaxing properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that often plague insomniacs. The oil's sedative effects promote a sense of tranquility, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. Additionally, hops essential oil has been shown to increase the production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter responsible for regulating sleep patterns, leading to a deeper and more restful sleep.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making them ideal for creating a calming essential oil for sleep.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh humulus lupulus leaves and stems at dawn on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours or until crispy.
Steam distill the dried humulus lupulus plant material with 100 ml of water for 30 minutes to extract essential oil.
Collect and filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper into a clean glass vial, yielding approximately 1 ml of oil.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil's gentle, sweet aroma has a direct impact on the mind, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. When inhaled or applied topically, chamomile oil can slow down an overactive mind, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate physical discomforts that may be disrupting sleep, creating a peaceful environment conducive to a restful night's sleep.

Sedative property: The sedative properties of chamomile essential oil help to promote relaxation, reduce muscle tension, and quiet the mind, allowing for a restful night's sleep.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to promote relaxation and calmness, helping to reduce stress and anxiety associated with sleep deprivation.

Flowers: The flowers contain a high concentration of azulene, a sesquiterpene that is responsible for the calming and sedative effects of chamomile essential oil, making it effective for promoting restful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated weighing approximately one pound.
Cleanly dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator set at 150 degrees fahrenheit for six hours.
Steam distill the dried chamomile flowers with distilled water in a 1:4 ratio for two and a half hours yielding seven ounces.
Filter the resulting essential oil from the distillation process through a coffee filter or paper towel into dark glass bottles.
Store the filtered matricaria chamomilla essential oil in a cool dark place away from direct sunlight for a minimum of six months prior to use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat sleep deprivation with herbal essential oils is by utilizing calming and sedative properties of Lavender, Chamomile, and Valerian root oil. Apply a few drops of each oil blend to your pillowcase or inhale through steam inhalation before bed. The soothing effects will help reduce stress and anxiety, promoting a restful night's sleep.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sleep deprivation that are treated with herbal essential oils are anxiety, stress, and insomnia. Bergamot oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing feelings of anxiety and promoting relaxation. Lavender oil, known for its calming properties, can help soothe an overactive mind and induce a restful night's sleep. Additionally, Roman Chamomile oil can help reduce restlessness and promote relaxation, making it an effective natural remedy for these ailments.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because of its calming and relaxing properties. The oil's sedative effects promote a sense of tranquility and reduce stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep. The aroma of lavender oil is known to slow down the heart rate and lower blood pressure, creating a peaceful environment conducive to sleep. remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which are common contributing factors to sleep deprivation.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits sedative properties, helping to calm the mind and body, and reducing stress levels associated with sleep deprivation.
Caryophyllene: Possesses anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of sleep deprivation, such as restlessness and muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the mind and body.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the lavandula angustifolia flowers in full bloom when they are in their prime condition for maximum oil content.
Clean the flowers thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris by gently rinsing them with water.
Steam distill the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours to release the oil.
Separate the essential oil from the water by decanting the oil into a separate container after distillation is complete.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and achieve a clear liquid consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because of its natural sedative properties. The oil contains valerenic acid, which interacts with the brain's GABA receptors to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. This leads to a decrease in stress hormones and an increase in melatonin production, helping to regulate the body's sleep-wake cycle. The calming effects of Valeriana officinalis essential oils also help to quiet the mind and prepare the body for a restful night's sleep, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps with sleep deprivation by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
Valeranone: It helps with sleep deprivation by exhibiting sedative and hypnotic properties, which can aid in falling asleep and improving sleep quality.
Valeranone acetate: It helps with sleep deprivation by contributing to the sedative effects of Valeriana officinalis, which can help reduce restlessness and promote a restful sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are the most commonly used part for essential oil production due to their high valerenic acid content, which helps promote relaxation and sleep.
Flowers: Valerian flowers are often used to create a milder, sweeter essential oil that can be used to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety without inducing drowsiness.
Stems: Valerian stems can be used to create a less potent essential oil that still offers some sedative properties, often used in combination with other oils for a balanced effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and leaves in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Steam distill the valeriana officinalis roots and leaves with a 1:1 ratio for 3-4 hours to release the oil.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distillate and transfer it to a glass container.
Filter the essential oil using a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and debris for 30 minutes.
Store the valeriana officinalis essential oil in a cool dark place at a 30% concentration in a glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because it contains a unique blend of bioactive compounds, including linalool and rosmarinic acid, which promote relaxation and reduce anxiety levels. The calming and soothing effects of Melissa officinalis essential oils can help regulate the body's sleep-wake cycle, allowing for a restful night's sleep. Additionally, its adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, promoting a sense of balance and tranquility, ultimately leading to improved sleep quality.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with sleep deprivation by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety levels, making it easier to fall asleep.
Linalyl acetate: This ester is known for its sedative properties, which help calm the mind and body, leading to improved sleep quality.
Borneol: This terpene has a sedative effect, helping to reduce stress and anxiety, and promoting a peaceful sleep.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in melissa oil, which contains compounds that have a calming effect on the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Flowers: The flowers of Melissa officinalis contain a higher concentration of melissic acid, a compound known for its sedative properties and ability to aid in relaxation.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also used to produce essential oils, which contain a blend of compounds that help to calm the nervous system and promote a restful night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis flowers in mid-summer when they are in full bloom for optimal potency and fragrance.
Steam distill 10 kg of freshly harvested melissa officinalis flowers with 3 liters of water for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separatory funnel or a centrifuge for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter paper to remove any impurities and debris for 20 minutes.
Store the final melissa officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because it promotes relaxation and calms the mind, reducing anxiety and stress that can interfere with sleep. Isovitexin: Isovitexin helps with sleep deprivation by acting as a sedative and anxiolytic, reducing stress and promoting a calm state.
Isoorientin: Isoorientin helps with sleep deprivation by exhibiting anti-anxiety properties and modulating the body's sleep-wake cycle through its effects on the central nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high levels of flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to their sedative properties.
Flowers: The essential oil extracted from Passiflora incarnata flowers has been traditionally used to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning when dew is still present.
Dry the flowers in a warm place for 2-3 days or until they reach 10% moisture content.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a 1:10 ratio with distilled water for 2-3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separating funnel within 30 minutes.
Store the passiflora incarnata essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because it promotes relaxation and calms the mind and body. remedy_constituents: Lignans: These polyphenolic compounds have sedative and anxiolytic effects, helping to calm the mind and body to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
Furofuran lignans (e.g., gramine and avenalumine): These compounds have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can lead to sleep deprivation.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has been found to have a relaxing effect on the body, reducing inflammation and promoting a sense of calmness that can help with falling asleep and staying asleep.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in tryptophan, an amino acid converted into serotonin and melatonin, promoting relaxation and sleep.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh avena sativa leaves and flowers at dawn when dew is present.
Steam distill the plant material in a 5-liter still with water at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid at 10°c.
Label and date the bottle and store it in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight.

Caryophyllene oxide: Exhibits a calming effect and helps in reducing inflammation, which can contribute to sleep deprivation caused by chronic pain or discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh anthemis nobilis flowers when they are in full bloom during the summer months.
Steam distill the harvested flowers at 100°c for 3-4 hours to obtain essential oil.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the water and plant material using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and particles.

This natural remedy supports a restful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps with sleep deprivation by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety through its sedative and calming effects.
α-Santalol: A sesquiterpene alcohol that aids in sleep by reducing stress and promoting relaxation due to its anxiolytic and calming properties.
Geraniol: An alcohol derived from terpenes that helps with sleep by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Santalum album for essential oils, as they contain high concentrations of sandalwood oil, which has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.

Barks: Santalum album barks are also widely used for essential oils, as they contain a significant amount of santalol, a compound that helps to reduce stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.
Leaves: The leaves of Santalum album are used for essential oils due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which have a calming effect on the mind and body, promoting relaxation and sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh santalum album leaves and twigs in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at a temperature of 100c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container using a separatory funnel to separate the oil from the water.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any remaining impurities and particles.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality and aroma.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because of its calming properties and natural ability to regulate the body's circadian rhythms. The oil contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. By applying Zingiber officinale essential oils to the skin or inhaling its aroma, individuals can experience a decrease in anxiety and a sense of calm, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene helps with sleep deprivation by promoting relaxation and reducing stress due to its sedative properties.
Gingerol: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of insomnia and promote a restful sleep.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can aid in falling asleep.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Underground stem structure - The essential oils extracted from rhyzomes of Zingiber officinale are commonly used to aid in sleep due to their calming and sedative properties.
Roots: Subterranean plant base - The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make essential oils for sleep deprivation, as they contain compounds that promote relaxation.
Buds: Immature flower structures - Zingiber officinale buds are occasionally used to produce essential oils that help alleviate sleep disorders due to their soothing effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes when the plant is in full bloom for maximum potency.
Steam distill the harvested rhizomes in a 50g batch for 3 hours using 5 liters of distilled water.
Collect 10ml of the resulting distillate and mix it with 90ml of a carrier oil such as jojoba oil.
Store the mixture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for 48 hours to allow for aging.
Filter the final product through a 0.2 micron filter paper into a clean glass container for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because of its calming and soothing properties. This oil, also known as German chamomile, promotes relaxation by reducing anxiety and stress levels. The apigenin content in Chamomilla recutita binds to GABA receptors in the brain, producing a calming effect. As a result, it helps regulate the body's sleep-wake cycle, leading to improved sleep quality and duration. Regular use of this essential oil can aid in overcoming sleep deprivation and promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as a GABA receptor agonist, helping to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, which can lead to improved sleep quality.
Alpha-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, reducing anxiety and promoting a peaceful state, making it easier to fall asleep.
Linalool: This terpene has a sedative effect, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of sleep deprivation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Chamomilla recutita are used to make essential oils due to their calming and soothing properties, which help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.

Leaves: The leaves of Chamomilla recutita are used to make essential oils due to their mild sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce stress and anxiety related to sleep deprivation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 pounds of fresh flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal essential oil content.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 60 degrees fahrenheit for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill 500 grams of dried flowers for 2 to 3 hours to extract the oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or decanter.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with sleep deprivation because it promotes relaxation and calms the mind. The oil's active compounds, bilobalide and ginkgolides, have a sedative effect, reducing anxiety and stress that often interfere with sleep. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help regulate the body's internal clock, allowing for a deeper and more restful sleep. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the brain, Ginkgo biloba essential oil supports a peaceful night's sleep and improved overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which in turn helps alleviate sleep deprivation symptoms.
Ginkgolides: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to sleep disturbances.
Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): Possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce stress and promote a calming effect, ultimately leading to improved sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree are most commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are known for their calming and sedative properties.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used for their sedative and sleep-promoting properties, often extracted and incorporated into sleep-promoting blends.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree are sometimes used in aromatherapy for their calming and grounding effects, which can help alleviate stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer to early fall.
Steam distill the leaves with 2 cups of water at 212°f for 2-3 hours to extract the oil.
Collect the distilled oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
Check the oil after 24 hours and discard any that has not solidified into a clear liquid.
